checks.describe: core-internals profile is up: kind context kind-kubeadv-core answers and the real worker node exists (CLUSTER KEPT — M5 continues on it)
the in-cluster KWOK controller Deployment is present and reporting available replicas (kwok-setup.sh deployed it)
checks.run: kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core -n kube-system get deploy kwok-controller -o jsonpath='avail={.status.availableReplicas}'; echo

checks.describe: KWOK created three fake nodes (labelled type=kwok) that report Ready to the scheduler
checks.run: N=$(kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core get nodes -l type=kwok --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c ' Ready'); echo "fakenodes=${N}"

checks.describe: a pod requesting more CPU than any node stays Pending with PodScheduled=False and the scheduler's Unschedulable reason (self-seeded and removed by the check; tolerates the kwok taint so all nodes are candidates)
checks.run:
kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core create namespace m4-check --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core apply -f - >/dev/null; printf 'apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: toobigcheck
spec:
  tolerations:
  - key: kwok.x-k8s.io/node
    operator: Exists
    effect: NoSchedule
  containers:
  - name: pause
    image: registry.k8s.io/pause:3.9
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: "9000"
' | kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core apply -n m4-check -f - >/dev/null; sleep 5; kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core get pod toobigcheck -n m4-check -o jsonpath='{range .status.conditions[?(@.type=="PodScheduled")]}scheduled={.status} reason={.reason}{end}'; echo; kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core delete namespace m4-check --wait=false >/dev/null

checks.describe: the scheduler's FailedScheduling message on an over-requesting pod names Insufficient cpu — the operator-readable cause (self-seeded and removed by the check)
checks.run:
kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core create namespace m4-check2 --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core apply -f - >/dev/null; printf 'apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: reasoncheck
spec:
  tolerations:
  - key: kwok.x-k8s.io/node
    operator: Exists
    effect: NoSchedule
  containers:
  - name: pause
    image: registry.k8s.io/pause:3.9
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: "9000"
' | kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core apply -n m4-check2 -f - >/dev/null; sleep 5; kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core get events -n m4-check2 --field-selector reason=FailedScheduling -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.message}{"
"}{end}' | head -1; kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core delete namespace m4-check2 --wait=false >/dev/null

checks.describe: a custom NoExecute taint on a fake node makes the TaintManager evict a non-tolerating pod running there (self-seeded and removed by the check; proves the eviction path is live)
checks.run:
FN=$(kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core get nodes -l type=kwok -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}' 2>/dev/null); if [ -z "$FN" ]; then echo 'NO-FAKE-NODE'; else kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core create namespace m4-check3 --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core apply -f - >/dev/null; printf 'apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: evictcheck
spec:
  nodeName: %s
  tolerations:
  - key: kwok.x-k8s.io/node
    operator: Exists
    effect: NoSchedule
  containers:
  - name: pause
    image: registry.k8s.io/pause:3.9
' "$FN" | kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core apply -n m4-check3 -f - >/dev/null; kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core wait --for=condition=Ready pod/evictcheck -n m4-check3 --timeout=30s >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core taint node "$FN" m4evict=check:NoExecute --overwrite >/dev/null; sleep 5; kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core get events -n m4-check3 --field-selector reason=TaintManagerEviction -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.reason}{"
"}{end}' | head -1; kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core taint node "$FN" m4evict- >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; kubectl --context kind-kubeadv-core delete namespace m4-check3 --wait=false >/dev/null; fi
